chapter_title: Forming Effective Human-AI Teams: Building Machine Learning Models that Complement the Capabilities of Multiple Experts

sentences.source_text: Forming Effective Human-AI Teams: Building Machine Learning Models that Complement the Capabilities of Multiple Experts
target_texts: 构建有效的人机协作团队：开发能够补充多位专家能力的机器学习模型

sentences.source_text: Patrick Hemmer 1 , Sebastian Schellhammer 1 ; 2 , Michael Vossing 1 , Johannes Jakubik 1 and Gerhard Satzger 1 1 Karlsruhe Institute of Technology 2 GESIS - Leibniz Institute for the Social Sciencesf patrick.hemmer , michael.v oessing, johannes.jakubik, gerhard.satzgerg @kit.edu, sebastian.schellhammer@gesis.org Abstract Machine learning ML models are increasingly be- ing used in application domains that often in volve working together with humane xperts.
target_texts: Patrick Hemmer 1, Sebastian Schellhammer 1;2, Michael Vössing 1, Johannes Jakubik 1 和 Gerhard Satzger 1 1 卡尔斯鲁厄理工学院 2 GESIS - 莱布尼茨社会科学研究所 f patrick.hemmer, michael.v oessing, johannes.jakubik, gerhard.satzgerg @kit.edu, sebastian.schellhammer@gesis.org 摘要 机器学习（ML）模型正越来越多地应用于通常涉及与人类专家协作的应用领域。

sentences.source_text: In this con-te xt,it can be adv antageous to defer certain in- stances to a single humane xpert when the y are dif- cult to predict for the ML model.
target_texts: 在此背景下，当某些实例对机器学习模型难以预测时，将其委托给单个人类专家可能是有利的。

sentences.source_text: While previ- ousw ork has focused on scenarios with one distinct humane xpert, in many real-w orld situations sev- eral humane xperts with varying capabilities may be a vailable.
target_texts: 虽然先前的研究主要关注仅有一位特定人类专家的场景，但在许多现实世界情境中，可能有多位能力各异的人类专家可供使用。

sentences.source_text: In this w ork, we propose an approach that trains a classi cation model to complem ent the capabilities of multiple humane xperts.
target_texts: 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种训练分类模型以补充多位人类专家能力的方法。

sentences.source_text: By jointly training the classi ertogether with an allocation system, the classi erlearns to accurately predict those instances that are dif cult for the humane x-perts, while the allocation system learns to pass each instance to the most suitable team member either the classi er or one of the humane xperts.
target_texts: 通过联合训练分类器与分配系统，分类器学会准确预测那些对人类专家而言困难的实例，而分配系统则学会将每个实例传递给最合适的团队成员（分类器或某位人类专家）。

sentences.source_text: Weev aluate our proposed approach in multiplee xper - iments on public datasets with synthetice xperts and a real-w orld medical dataset annotated by mul- tiple radiologists.
target_texts: 我们在多个公开数据集上通过合成专家和由多位放射科医生标注的真实世界医学数据集进行了多项实验，以评估我们提出的方法。

sentences.source_text: Our approach outperforms priorw ork and ismore accurate than the best humane x-pert or a classi er .
target_texts: 我们的方法优于先前的研究，并且比最佳人类专家或分类器更准确。

sentences.source_text: Furthermore, itis exibly adapt- able to teams of varying sizes and dif ferent lev els of expert div ersity .
target_texts: 此外，该方法能灵活适应不同规模的团队和不同水平的专家多样性。

sentences.source_text: 1 Intr oduction Over the last years, the performance of machine learning ML models has become comparable to the one of humane xperts in a rising number of application domains [ Estev a etal. , 2017 ] .
target_texts: 1 引言 近年来，机器学习（ML）模型的性能在越来越多的应用领域中已变得可与人类专家相媲美[Esteva et al., 2017]。

sentences.source_text: For example, in medici ne,se veral studies hav edemonstrated that ML models can surpass the performance of radiologists for the diagnosis of pneumonia in chest X- rays [ Irvin etal. , 2019 ] or achiev esimilar performance for the detection of diabetic retinopathy [ Gulshan etal. , 2019 ] .
target_texts: 例如，在医学领域，多项研究已证明机器学习模型在胸部X光肺炎诊断方面可以超越放射科医生的表现[Irvin et al., 2019]，或在糖尿病视网膜病变检测方面达到相似性能[Gulshan et al., 2019]。

sentences.source_text: Despite these competitiv eresults, indi viduale xperts are of- ten still more accurate than ML algo rithms in distinct areas of the inputs pace [ Raghu etal. , 2019 ] , e.g., due to lim- ited model capacity , limited training data, or the existence These authors contrib uted equally . of side information that is not accessible to the ML model [ Wilder etal. , 2020 ] .
target_texts: 尽管有这些具有竞争力的结果，个体专家在输入空间的特定区域通常仍然比机器学习算法更准确[Raghu et al., 2019]，例如，由于模型容量有限、训练数据有限，或者存在机器学习模型无法访问的辅助信息[Wilder et al., 2020]。

sentences.source_text: In this conte xt,priorw ork has in- vestig ated whether these dif ferent capabilities can be lever - aged to enable ML models to complement the weaknesses of a single humane xpert by learning to predict only a frac- tion of the instances and pass the remaining ones to the do-main expert [ Bansal etal. , 2021; Deet al. , 2021; Madras etal. , 2018; Mozannar and Sontag, 2020; Raghu etal. , 2019; Wilder etal. , 2020 ] .
target_texts: 在此背景下，先前的研究探讨了是否可以利用这些不同的能力，使机器学习模型通过学习仅预测一部分实例并将剩余实例传递给领域专家，来补充单个人类专家的弱点[Bansal et al., 2021; De et al., 2021; Madras et al., 2018; Mozannar and Sontag, 2020; Raghu et al., 2019; Wilder et al., 2020]。

sentences.source_text: Even though in many real-w orld appli- cations multiple humane xperts are a vailable for load sharing and cov ering dif ferent instances [ Chouldechov a etal. , 2018; Grnsund and Aanestad, 2020 ] , the majority of these stud- ies focus on settings in which ML models take the decision themselv es for a giv eninstance or leav eit to a single humane xpert.
target_texts: 尽管在许多现实世界应用中，有多位人类专家可用于负载分担和覆盖不同实例[Chouldechova et al., 2018; Grønsund and Aanestad, 2020]，但大多数研究仍关注机器学习模型自行决定给定实例或将其留给单个人类专家的场景。

sentences.source_text: Consequently , the ydo notle verage potential disagree- ment between indi vidual humane xperts due to div erse capa- bilities.
target_texts: 因此，它们未能利用由于能力多样而导致的个体人类专家之间的潜在分歧。

sentences.source_text: Thus, from a team perspective, the potential to im-prove the overall performance remains unused.
target_texts: 因此，从团队角度来看，提升整体性能的潜力仍未得到利用。

sentences.source_text: Dif ferences in the predictions of domaine xperts can,e.g., stem from dif- ferent lev els of kno wledge or personal biases [ Lampert etal. , 2016 ] .
target_texts: 领域专家预测的差异可能源于不同的知识水平或个人偏见[Lampert et al., 2016]。

sentences.source_text: Additionally , the rapid dev elopmentsi nhighly spe- cialized elds, e.g.,medicine, are making itincreasingly dif- cult for domaine xperts to acquire all kno wledge within a single specialization domain.
target_texts: 此外，在高度专业化领域（例如医学）的快速发展，使得领域专家越来越难以掌握单一专业领域内的所有知识。

sentences.source_text: This creates the need to identify the appropriate humane xpert to assign particular instances to when the y are dif cult to predict for the ML model.
target_texts: 这就需要在机器学习模型难以预测某些实例时，确定应将其分配给哪位合适的人类专家。

sentences.source_text: W e,therefore, propose an approach that jointly 1 trains a classi- er to speci cally complement the indi vidual weaknesses of multiple humane xperts and 2 trains an allocation system to assign each instance either to the classi er or one specic hu-mane xpert.
target_texts: 因此，我们提出了一种方法，该方法（1）联合训练一个分类器以专门补充多位人类专家的个体弱点，并（2）训练一个分配系统，将每个实例分配给分类器或某位特定的人类专家。

sentences.source_text: The setting with sev eral a vailable humane xperts and a classi eris referred to as a team.
target_texts: 拥有多位可用人类专家和一个分类器的场景被称为一个团队。

sentences.source_text: The team member to whom an instance isassigned makes then a prediction on be- half of the entire team.
target_texts: 被分配实例的团队成员随后代表整个团队做出预测。

sentences.source_text: Overall, our w ork makes the follo wing contrib utions: First, we present a nov elapproach that optimizes the per - formance of human-AI teams by jointly training a classi ertogether with an allocation system under consideration of the indi vidual capabilities of a vailable humane xperts.
target_texts: 总体而言，我们的工作做出了以下贡献：首先，我们提出了一种新颖的方法，该方法在考虑可用人类专家个体能力的情况下，联合训练分类器与分配系统，以优化人机协作团队的性能。

sentences.source_text: While the classi erlearns to accurately predict the areas of the input space in which humane xperts are less competent, the alloca- tion system learns to assign each instance to the best-suited team member .
target_texts: 当分类器学会准确预测人类专家能力较弱的输入空间区域时，分配系统则学会将每个实例分配给最合适的团队成员。

sentences.source_text: Second, we dem onstrate that our approa chimpro ves the
target_texts: 其次，我们证明我们的方法改进了

sentences.source_text: state-of-the-art by conducting e xperiments on tw o datasets with synthetically generated human e xperts\227a dataset con- sisting of tweets containing hate spee ch and of fensi v e lan- guage [ Da vidson et al. , 2017 ] and the popular CIF AR-100 dataset.
target_texts: 通过在两个包含合成生成人类专家的数据集上进行实验，我们展示了我们方法的最先进性能：一个包含仇恨言论和攻击性语言的推文数据集[Davidson et al., 2017]以及流行的CIFAR-100数据集。

sentences.source_text: W e sho w that our approach can not only deal with dif ferent team sizes b ut also better le v erages high hu- man e xpert di v ersity in terms of capabilities than prior w ork [ K esw ani et al. , 2021 ] .
target_texts: 我们表明，我们的方法不仅能处理不同的团队规模，而且比先前的工作[Keswani et al., 2021]能更好地利用人类专家在能力方面的高度多样性。

sentences.source_text: Moreo v er , we visually illustrate that the classi\002er in our approach mak es correct predictions for the instances where human e xperts are less accurate and that the allocation system learns to assign e xactly these instances to the classi\002er .
target_texts: 此外，我们通过可视化说明，在我们的方法中，分类器在人类专家准确度较低的实例上做出正确预测，并且分配系统学会将这些实例恰好分配给分类器。

sentences.source_text: Third, we demonstrate the real-w orld applicability of our approach with an e xample from the medical domain.
target_texts: 第三，我们通过一个来自医学领域的例子，展示了我们方法的实际应用性。

sentences.source_text: The Chest X-ray dataset [ Majk o wska et al. , 2020; W ang et al. , 2017 ] of the NIH Clinical Center pro vides a suitable s etting as the annotations do not only include radiologist-adjudicated labels, which serv e as a high-quality \223gold standard\224, b ut also the annotations on an indi vidual radiologist le v el.
target_texts: NIH临床中心的胸部X光数据集[Majkowska et al., 2020; Wang et al., 2017]提供了一个合适的场景，因为其标注不仅包括放射科医生裁决的标签（作为高质量的“金标准”），还包括个体放射医生级别的标注。

sentences.source_text: In this con- te xt, prior w ork has conducted e xperimental e v aluations on datasets that either pro vide only a distrib ution of human e x- pert labels, e.g., CIF AR-10H [ Peterson et al. , 2019 ] or labels that can not be traced back to indi vidual human e xperts, e.g., Galaxy Zoo dataset [ Lintott et al. , 2008 ] .
target_texts: 在此背景下，先前的工作已在数据集上进行了实验评估，这些数据集要么仅提供人类专家标签的分布（例如CIFAR-10H [Peterson et al., 2019]），要么提供无法追溯到个体人类专家的标签（例如Galaxy Zoo数据集[Lintott et al., 2008]）。

sentences.source_text: W e pro vide our code and the Appendix of this paper at https://github .com/ptrckhmmr/human- ai- teams.
target_texts: 我们在https://github.com/ptrckhmmr/human-ai-teams提供我们的代码和本文附录。

sentences.source_text: 2 Related W ork In recent years, there has been gro wing interest in optimiz- ing the performance of human-AI teams\227teams consisting of both a human e xpert and a classi\002er [ Hemmer et al. , 2021 ] \227 by allocating a subset of the instances to a single human e xpert [ De et al. , 2021; K esw ani et al. , 2021; Madras et al. , 2018; Mozannar and Sontag, 2020; Okati et al. , 2021; Raghu et al. , 2019; W ilder et al. , 2020 ] .
target_texts: 2 相关工作 近年来，通过将一部分实例分配给单个人类专家来优化人机协作团队（由人类专家和分类器组成的团队）[Hemmer et al., 2021]的性能引起了越来越多的兴趣[De et al., 2021; Keswani et al., 2021; Madras et al., 2018; Mozannar and Sontag, 2020; Okati et al., 2021; Raghu et al., 2019; Wilder et al., 2020]。

sentences.source_text: In this conte xt, Raghu et al. [ 2019 ] separately train a classi\002er and a model for predicting whether a human e xpert errs.
target_texts: 在此背景下，Raghu等人[2019]分别训练了一个分类器和一个用于预测人类专家是否出错的模型。

sentences.source_text: By deferring based on which of the tw o models has higher uncertainty , superior team performance can be achie v ed that surpasses both full automation \050i.e., the classi\002er predicts all instances\051 and sole human e f fort \050i.e., the human e xpert predicts all in- stances\051.
target_texts: 通过基于两个模型中哪一个具有更高不确定性进行移交，可以实现超越完全自动化（即分类器预测所有实例）和纯人工努力（即人类专家预测所有实例）的优异团队性能。

sentences.source_text: While the authors train the classi\002er in isolation to optimize its performance, dif ferent studies emphasize that the classi\002er could also be trained to speci\002cally complement the human e xpert' s strengths and weaknesses.
target_texts: 尽管作者单独训练分类器以优化其性能，但不同研究强调，分类器也可以被训练来专门补充人类专家的优势和弱点。

sentences.source_text: Se v eral ap- proaches propose to jointly train a cla ssi\002er and a deferral system [ Madras et al. , 2018; W ilder et al. , 2020 ] .
target_texts: 几种方法提出联合训练一个分类器和一个移交系统[Madras et al., 2018; Wilder et al., 2020]。

sentences.source_text: Other ap- proaches utilize objecti v e functions with theoretical guaran- tees [ De et al. , 2021; Mozannar and Sontag, 2020 ] .
target_texts: 其他方法则采用具有理论保证的目标函数[De et al., 2021; Mozannar and Sontag, 2020]。

sentences.source_text: De et al. [ 2021 ] focus on support v ector machines and maximize sub- modular functions to optimize team performance.
target_texts: De等人[2021]专注于支持向量机，并通过最大化子模函数来优化团队性能。

sentences.source_text: Mozannar and Sontag [ 2020 ] optimize a consistent surrog ate loss de- ri v ed from cost-sensiti v e learning.
target_texts: Mozannar和Sontag[2020]优化了一个源自成本敏感学习的一致性代理损失。

sentences.source_text: Further related w ork has in v estig ated optimal triage policies [ Okati et al. , 2021 ] , user - initiated defer options [ Bansal et al. , 2021 ] , or learning com- plementarity from bandit feedback [ Gao et al. , 2021 ] .
target_texts: 其他相关研究调查了最优分诊策略[Okati et al., 2021]、用户发起的移交选项[Bansal et al., 2021]，或从老虎机反馈中学习互补性[Gao et al., 2021]。

sentences.source_text: In gen- eral, these approaches focus on combining a classi\002er with a single human e xpert.
target_texts: 总的来说，这些方法侧重于将分类器与单个人类专家相结合。

sentences.source_text: Ho we v er , in reality , more than one human e xpert is often a v ailable [ Chouldecho v a et al. , 2018; Gr\370nsund and Aanestad, 2020 ] .
target_texts: 然而，在现实中，往往不止一位人类专家可用[Chouldechova et al., 2018; Grønsund and Aanestad, 2020]。

sentences.source_text: Here b y , it can be of par - ticular interest in resource-scarce domains to consult e xactly the right e xpert to a v oid potentially costly mistak es.
target_texts: 因此，在资源稀缺的领域中，咨询恰好合适的专家以避免潜在代价高昂的错误可能具有特殊意义。

sentences.source_text: Our ap- proach e xtends the idea that the classi\002er should complement the strengt hs and weaknesses of a single human e xpert to a setting with multiple human e xperts.
target_texts: 我们的方法将分类器应补充单个人类专家优缺点的理念扩展到了具有多位人类专家的场景。

sentences.source_text: The system allocates in- stances to one e xpert of a team consisting of multiple human e xperts and a complementary classi\002er .
target_texts: 该系统将实例分配给一个由多位人类专家和一个互补分类器组成的团队中的一位专家。

sentences.source_text: Concurrent w ork pro- poses a multi-label classi\002cation approach in which a classi- \002er either mak es a prediction or defers the instance to one of multiple human e xperts [ K esw ani et al. , 2021 ] .
target_texts: 同时期的工作提出了一种多标签分类方法，其中分类器要么进行预测，要么将实例移交给多位人类专家中的一位[Keswani et al., 2021]。

sentences.source_text: Ho we v er , the approach does not le v erage the human e xperts' disagreement to enhance the o v erall team performance b ut solely relies on identifying the best o v erall team member instead.
target_texts: 然而，该方法并未利用人类专家之间的分歧来提升整体团队性能，而仅仅依赖于识别最佳的整体团队成员。

sentences.source_text: 3 Pr oblem F ormulation In this section, we introduce the problem of optimizing the performance of human-AI teams on classi\002cation tasks.
target_texts: 3 问题表述 在本节中，我们介绍优化人机协作团队在分类任务上性能的问题。

target_texts: 给定一个训练示例x ∈ X，我们学习预测其真实标签y ∈ Y = {1, ..., k}，其中k表示类别数量。

sentences.source_text: In addition to y , we assume to ha v e access to the predictions h 2 H of m human e xperts for all training instances to implicitly le arn their strengths and weaknesses.
target_texts: 除了y之外，我们假设能够访问m位人类专家对所有训练实例的预测h ∈ H，以隐式学习他们的优势和弱点。

sentences.source_text: Each h is a m -dimensional v ector wit h h = [ h 1 ; : : : ; h m ] with h j 2 Y 8 j 2 f 1 ; : : : ; m g .
target_texts: 每个h是一个m维向量，h = [h_1, ..., h_m]，其中h_j ∈ Y，∀ j ∈ {1, ..., m}。

target_texts: 我们将这些数据组合成训练数据集D = {(x, y, h)}^N_1 ~ P，其中N表示数据实例的数量，以联合训练一个分配系统A: X → R^{m+1}和一个分类器F: X → R^k。

sentences.source_text: R m +1 and a classi\002er F : X !

target_texts: 我们将这些数据组合成训练数据集D = {(x, y, h)}^N_1 ~ P，其中N表示数据实例的数量，以联合训练一个分配系统A: X → R^{m+1}和一个分类器F: X → R^k。

target_texts: 这里，P是一个（未知的）联合分布，m+1表示人机协作团队的总规模，A和F的输出指的是各自模型的倒数第二层。

sentences.source_text: The classi\002er predicts ^ y cl f = arg max F \050 x \051 and the alloca- tion system indicates the competence of each team member including the classi\002er with a = A \050 x \051 for an instance x .
target_texts: 分类器预测^y_clf = arg max F(x)，而分配系统通过a = A(x)为实例x指示包括分类器在内的每个团队成员的胜任能力。

sentences.source_text: Combining the indi vidual predictions of all team members ^ y = [ h 1 ; : : : ; h m ; ^ y cl f ] and the inde x of the most competent team member j max = arg max a results in the team predic- tion ^ y team = ^ y j max .

sentences.source_text: The goal is then to minimize the team loss L team \050 F ; A; x ; y ; h \051 = E \050 x ;y ; h \051 \030 P \002 l \050 y ; ^ y team \051 \003 : \0501\051 T o minimize the loss, the classi\002er needs to be accurate on those instance s where the human e xperts are less accurate.
target_texts: 目标是最小化团队损失 L_team(F, A, x, y, h) = E_{(x,y,h)~P}[ℓ(y, ^y_team)] : (1)。为了最小化损失，分类器需要在人类专家准确率较低的实例上保持准确。

sentences.source_text: The allocation system' s function is to assign each instance to the team member with the highest probability of a correct prediction to answer on behalf of the team.

sentences.source_text: Figure 3: V isualization of the team members' performance\227human e xperts' accurac y in \050a\051 and classi\002er accurac y in \050b\051\227and instance assignment by the allocation system in \050c\051 on a subset of the CIF AR-100 input space. select m = 2 human e xperts o v er i 2 N runs. T o isolate the in\003uence of di v ersity , we v ary the capabilities of the second human e xpert per run while k eeping the ones of the \002rst \002x ed. F or i = 1 , we select the \002rst 90 subclasses f 1 ; : : : ; 90 g in alphabetical order for which both human e xperts predict the corresponding superclass perfectly . F or the remaining sub- classes, both e xperts predict uniformly at random across all superclasses. W e then v ary the competencies of the second human e xpert such that for run i the perfectly predicted sub- classes are f i; : : : ; 89 + i g . By increasing i per run by 1, the number of subclasses in which only one of both human e x- perts predicts perfectly increases by 2 subclasses resulting in a di v ersity increase. F or the third analysis, we resort to one of these di v ersity scenarios. Model. W e use a ResNet-18 model [ He et al. , 2016 ] pre- trained on ImageNet as a \002x ed feature e xtractor with 512 out- put units. These features are used to train our approach and the algorithmic baselines. The classi\002er and allocation sys- tem are modeled by a neural netw ork consisting of a single hidden layer with 100 units follo wed by a ReLU acti v ation. Experimental Setup. F or the \002rst analysis, we assess the performance of teams with 2 to 10 human e xperts with a nor - mally distrib uted number of perfectly predicted subclasses as pre viously described. F or the second analysis, we v ary i from 1 to 11 for the second human e xpert to gradually increase the number of non-o v erlapping subclasses. As a result, di v ersity is 0 for run i = 1 and 20 for run i = 11 . F or the third anal- ysis, we select one of the di v ersity scenarios\227namely where the \002rst human e xpert is de\002ned as f 1 ; : : : ; 90 g and the sec- ond one as f 6 ; : : : ; 95 g \227to visualize ho w instances are dis- trib uted among classi\002er and human e xperts relati v e to each team member' s respecti v e competencies. W e di vide the 50,000 training images into a training and v alidation split of 80% and 20% and use the 10,000 test im- ages as the test split. W e train the models for 100 epochs us- ing Adam optimizer with a learning rate of 5 \001 10 \000 3 , a cosine annealing learning rate scheduler , wei ght decay of 5 \001 10 \000 4 , and a batch size of 512. W e apply early stopping on the v ali- dation split and report the results on the test split. W e repeat the e xperiments 5 times with dif ferent seeds. Results. First, consistent with the results from Section 5.1, we \002nd that our approach bene\002ts fr o m an increasing num- ber of human e xperts. This is in line with our e xpectations as the probability that at least one e xpert has strengths in distinct subclasses or on all subclasses of a superclass is higher with an increasing number of human e xperts. In both cases, the classi\002er can focus on an e v en smaller area of the input space. On a v erage, our approach \050 Classi\002er & Expert T eam \051 outper - forms the JSF baseline by 19.77%, the Best Expert baseline by 19.73%, the One Classi\002er baseline by 25.86%, the Ran- dom Expert baseline by 30.75%, and the Classi\002er T eam by 24.88%. Ev en though the contrib ution from the classi\002er in our approach diminishes the more human e xperts become part of the team, it le v erages each indi vidual team member' s capa- bilities best. This can be observ ed from a performance g ain of 0.98% compared to the Expert T eam . W e refer to Figure A1 in Appendix A for an additional visualization. Second, we \002nd that for the Classi\002er & Expert T eam , a higher di v ersity of human team members' capabili ties con- trib utes to higher team performance as displayed in Figure 2. Ev en though the contrib ution of the classi\002er diminishes in the presence of increasingly di v erse human e xperts, it adds an a v erage performance g ain of 2.40% across all runs com- pared to the Expert T eam . Contrary , the JSF baseline does not bene\002t from a di v ersity increase b ut instead assigns the instances to the best human e xpert. Note that we aggre g ate the Best Expert and Random Expert baseline in Figure 2 as both human e xperts ha v e the same accurac y by design. Finally , we illustrate the capabilities of our approach in Figure 3: \0501\051 the classi\002er' s compensation for human e x- perts' weaknesses and \0502\051 the allocat ion system' s assignment of instances rel ati v e to each team member' s competencies. F or the visualization, we reduce the hidden layer' s features to 2 dimensi on s using the UMAP algorithm [ McInnes et al. , 2018 ] and aggre g ate a set of images in each he xagon. Fig- ure 3a and Figure 3b sho w the accurac y of 2 hu m an e xperts and the classi\002er , respecti v ely . In combination, the illustra- tions re v eal that the classi\002er primarily learns to optimize its performance on those images \050green he xagons in Figure 3b\051 where human e xperts tend to be less accurate \050yello w and red he xagons in Figure 3a\051. Figure 3c displays that the allocation system assigns those instances to the classi\002er where the hu- man e xperts are less accurate. Here, dark areas indicate the assignment of the images to the classi\002er and brighter areas denote the allocation to one of the human e xperts. 5.3 NIH Dataset Lastly , we e v aluate our approach on a real-w orld chest X-ray dataset collected by the NIH Clinical Center [ Majk o wska et al. , 2020; W ang et al. , 2017 ] . sentences.source_text: Contrary , for the NIH dataset, it remains e x-ante unclear to which de gree features are a ssociated with the human e xperts' competencies.
target_texts: 相反，对于NIH数据集，特征在何种程度上与人类专家的能力相关联，事前并不明确。

sentences.source_text: Dataset.
target_texts: 数据集。

sentences.source_text: The dataset comprises radiologists' annotations for 4 radiographic \002ndings on 4,374 chest X-ray images from the ChestX-ray8 dataset [ Majk o wska et al. , 2020; W ang et al. , 2017 ] .
target_texts: 该数据集包含放射科医生对ChestX-ray8数据集中4,374张胸部X光图像的4种放射学发现的标注[Majkowska等人，2020；Wang等人，2017]。

sentences.source_text: W e focus on the occurrence of the clinically im- portant \002nding air space opacity which has a pre v alence \050i.e., the percentage of patients in the dataset that is af fected with this disease\051 of 49.50%.
target_texts: 我们关注临床上重要的发现——空气空间混浊的发生，其患病率（即数据集中受此疾病影响的患者百分比）为49.50%。

sentences.source_text: F or each X-ray image, the ground- truth label w as adjudicated by a panel of 3 radiologists serv- ing as the \223gold standard\224 label.
target_texts: 对于每张X光图像，真实标签由3名放射科医生组成的专家组裁定，作为“金标准”标签。

sentences.source_text: Additionally , the annotations of each of the three radiologists from a cohort of 22 human e xperts are reported per image.
target_texts: 此外，每张图像还报告了来自22名人类专家队列中三名放射科医生各自的标注。

sentences.source_text: Each radiologist can be iden- ti\002ed by a unique ID of which we list t he last three digits due to space constraints.
target_texts: 每位放射科医生可通过唯一ID识别，由于空间限制，我们列出其最后三位数字。

sentences.source_text: Model.
target_texts: 模型。

sentences.source_text: W e use a ResNet-18 model pretrained on the CheX- pert dataset\227a dif ferent X-ray dataset for chest radiograph interpretation [ Irvin et al. , 2019 ] \227which serv es as a \002x ed feature e xtractor with 512 output units.
target_texts: 我们使用在CheXpert数据集上预训练的ResNet-18模型——这是一个用于胸部X光片解读的不同X射线数据集[Irvin等人，2019]——作为具有512个输出单元的固定特征提取器。

sentences.source_text: F or our approach and the algorithmic baselines, we use these features for model training.
target_texts: 对于我们的方法和算法基线，我们使用这些特征进行模型训练。

sentences.source_text: The classi\002er and allocation system are modeled by a single hidden layer neural netw ork with 30 units and a ReLU acti v ation function.
target_texts: 分类器和分配系统由一个具有30个单元和ReLU激活函数的单隐藏层神经网络建模。

sentences.source_text: Experimental Setup.
target_texts: 实验设置。

sentences.source_text: W e conduct e xperiments with 4 pairs of radiologists that share more than 800 images labeled by both radiologists.
target_texts: 我们使用4对放射科医生进行实验，这些放射科医生共享超过800张由双方标注的图像。

sentences.source_text: Due to insuf \002cient intersection, a third ra- diologist could not be included in the team.
target_texts: 由于交集不足，第三名放射科医生无法纳入团队。

sentences.source_text: W e perform a 10-fold cross-v alidation while ensuring that images from one patient are not spread across dif ferent folds.
target_texts: 我们执行10折交叉验证，同时确保来自同一患者的图像不会分散到不同的折中。

sentences.source_text: T o k eep the per fold performance of the radiologists close to their o v erall per - formance, we use strati\002cation.
target_texts: 为了保持放射科医生在每个折上的性能接近其整体性能，我们使用分层抽样。

sentences.source_text: F or each cross-v alidation run, we use 7 folds for training and 2 f o l ds for v alidation to per - form early stopping as the team performance on one v alida- tion fold is not al w ays a good proxy for the team performance on the test fold due to the small dataset size.
target_texts: 对于每次交叉验证运行，我们使用7个折进行训练，2个折进行验证以执行早停，因为由于数据集规模较小，团队在一个验证折上的性能并不总是测试折上团队性能的良好代理。

sentences.source_text: The remaining fold serv es as the t est data.
target_texts: 剩余的折作为测试数据。

sentences.source_text: W e train the models for 20 epochs using Adam optimizer with a learning rate of 1 \001 10 \000 3 , weight decay of 5 \001 10 \000 4 , and a batch size of 64.
target_texts: 我们使用Adam优化器训练模型20个周期，学习率为1×10⁻³，权重衰减为5×10⁻⁴，批量大小为64。

sentences.source_text: W e repeat the e x- periments 5 times with dif ferent seeds.
target_texts: 我们使用不同的种子重复实验5次。

sentences.source_text: Results.
target_texts: 结果。

sentences.source_text: T able 1 compares the performance of our approach with the baselines.
target_texts: 表1比较了我们的方法与基线的性能。

sentences.source_text: In detail, the performance of our approach \050 Classi\002er & Expert T eam \051 outperforms the other baselines.
target_texts: 具体而言，我们的方法（分类器与专家团队）的性能优于其他基线。

sentences.source_text: Additionally , the team members' performances on the sub- sets not assigned to them are displayed.
target_texts: 此外，还展示了团队成员在未分配给他们的子集上的表现。

sentences.source_text: P articularly , in high-stak es decision-making \050e.g., medicine\051, the performance impro v ements can contrib ute to further re- ducing potentially se v ere consequences of misdiagnoses.
target_texts: 特别是在高风险决策（如医疗领域）中，性能提升有助于进一步减少误诊可能导致的严重后果。

sentences.source_text: A closer look at the subset of images assigned to each human e xpert and the classi\002er sho ws that the performance of each team member is highest on the subset assigned to them.
target_texts: 仔细观察分配给每位人类专家和分类器的图像子集可以发现，每位团队成员在其被分配的子集上表现最佳。

sentences.source_text: T a- ble 2 illustrates this allocation by the diagonal v alues using the e xample of the human-AI team [ F , 357, 117].
target_texts: 表2通过人类-AI团队[F, 357, 117]的示例，使用对角线值说明了这种分配。

sentences.source_text: Thus, the classi\002er learns and is assigned to an area of the input space where it is more accurate than the radiologists.
target_texts: 因此，分类器学习并被分配到输入空间中比放射科医师更准确的区域。

sentences.source_text: Further - more, the allocation system assigns the remaining instances between the tw o radiologists based on their competencies.
target_texts: 此外，分配系统根据两位放射科医师的能力，在两者之间分配剩余实例。

sentences.source_text: 6 Conclusion In t his w ork, we le v erage the complementary capabilities of human e xperts and AI models by jointly training a classi\002er together with an allocation system.
target_texts: 6 结论 在这项工作中，我们通过联合训练分类器与分配系统，利用人类专家和AI模型的互补能力。

sentences.source_text: While the classi\002er aug- ments human e xperts' weaknesses, the allocation system as- signs instances to either one of the human e xperts or the clas- si\002er bas ed on their indi vidual capabilities.
target_texts: 分类器弥补人类专家的弱点，而分配系统则根据个体能力将实例分配给其中一位人类专家或分类器。

sentences.source_text: W e pro vide e x- perimental e vidence with synthetic and real human e xpert an- notations for the ability of such teams to not only outperform prior w ork b ut also to achie v e superior performance results none of the team members could ha v e accomplished alone.
target_texts: 我们通过合成和真实人类专家标注的实验证据表明，此类团队不仅能够超越先前工作，还能取得任何团队成员单独无法实现的卓越性能结果。

sentences.source_text: Furthermore, we sho w that our approach can cope with dif fer - ent de grees of human e xpert di v ersity and generally bene\002ts from a higher number of human team members.
target_texts: 此外，我们表明我们的方法能够应对不同程度的人类专家多样性，并且通常受益于更多人类团队成员。

sentences.source_text: As we do not constrain the a v ailability and capacity of e x pe rts at run time, opportunities for future research could e xplore load balanc- ing strate gies or alternati v e assignment strate gies if the most accurate team member is not a v ailable.
target_texts: 由于我们在运行时未限制专家的可用性和容量，未来研究机会可探索负载均衡策略或替代分配策略，以防最准确的团队成员不可用。

sentences.source_text: Las tly , we assume access to the human e xperts' predictions for all training data, implying a signi\002cant manual labeling ef fort.
target_texts: 最后，我们假设能够获取人类专家对所有训练数据的预测，这意味着需要大量手动标注工作。

sentences.source_text: T o address this limitation, further w ork could e xtend our approach to le v er - age data that is not annotated by all human e xperts in a team.
target_texts: 为应对这一限制，未来工作可将我们的方法扩展至利用团队中并非所有人类专家都标注的数据。

sentences.source_text: Ethical Statement As this w ork pursues the training of an algorithm that aug- ments the weaknesses of human e xperts, it is subject to im- portant ethical considerations.
target_texts: 伦理声明 由于这项工作旨在训练一种增强人类专家弱点的算法，因此涉及重要的伦理考量。

sentences.source_text: Our goal is to realize impro v ed performance compared to full automation and sole human ef- fort which can be bene\002cial in high-stak es decision-making.
target_texts: 我们的目标是实现比完全自动化和仅靠人类努力更优的性能，这在高风险决策中可能有益。

sentences.source_text: Ho we v er , the approach could theoretically be used to estimate the contrib ution of indi vidual team members to the team' s o v erall performance.
target_texts: 然而，该方法理论上可用于估计个体团队成员对团队整体表现的贡献。

sentences.source_text: This could be used to e xclude insuf \002- ciently performing e xperts from the team.
target_texts: 这可用于将表现不足的专家排除在团队之外。

sentences.source_text: Ho we v er , identi- fying the weaknesses of indi vidual e xperts also pro vides an opportunity to educate them through tar geted training.
target_texts: 然而，识别个体专家的弱点也提供了通过针对性培训教育他们的机会。
